name: Type 1 Diabetes - Diagnosis Code Query (Multi-Subtype, T1-vs-T2 Distinction)
description: Tests ability to find Type 1 diabetes patients via diagnosis codes — distinct from Type 2 diabetes (already in suite). T1D is autoimmune, requires insulin from onset; T2D is insulin-resistance-based, often managed without insulin initially. Tests whether LLM correctly distinguishes T1D SNOMED 46635009 from T2D SNOMED 44054006. Patients in EHRs are sometimes miscoded between the two — this is the FIRST subtype-vs-subtype test in the suite.
prompt: Find all patients with a diagnosis of type 1 diabetes mellitus, including insulin-dependent diabetes mellitus (IDDM) variants.

prompts.naive: Find all patients with type 1 diabetes.
prompts.broad: Find all patients diagnosed with type 1 diabetes mellitus (T1DM) — including insulin-dependent diabetes mellitus type IA, T1D with ulcer, pre-existing T1D, and T1D without retinopathy. NOT type 2 diabetes — these are distinct conditions with different SNOMED codes.
prompts.expert: I need to identify all type 1 diabetes patients. T1D is documented under multiple SNOMED codes: Diabetes mellitus type 1 (46635009) is the parent, with subtype variants Insulin dependent diabetes mellitus type IA (23045005), Type I diabetes mellitus with ulcer (190368000), Pre-existing type 1 diabetes mellitus (199229001), and Diabetes mellitus type 1 without retinopathy (31321000119102). Important: T1D and T2D are distinct conditions with different SNOMED codes — T2D parent is 44054006, NOT 46635009. Search Condition resources for the T1D codes only.

metadata.algorithm_path_description: Patients with T1D diagnosis codes (Paths A and B). Tests subtype distinction from T2D — an LLM that uses T2D codes will return zero T1D patients.

required_codes.display: Insulin dependent diabetes mellitus type IA
Type I diabetes mellitus with ulcer
